text.title: writing-implementation-plans Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for writing-implementation-plans as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Writing the implementation plan before design.md reaches Accepted status.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Draft designs change. Plans written on Draft designs must be rewritten when requirements shift during design review.

failure-mode.description: Omitting test-writing tasks and CHANGELOG updates from the WBS.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These tasks consume 20-40k tokens each but are consistently omitted. They must appear as explicit tasks with token estimates.

failure-mode.description: Implementation plan contradicting design.md on task scope or file targets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents silently follow the impl-plan when it contradicts design.md. Reconcile both documents before execution begins — the discrepancy may indicate a design error.

failure-mode.description: Token estimates are order-of-magnitude only with no breakdown.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unitemized estimates run 2-3x over in practice. Itemized estimates by file class are diagnosable when they overrun.
